onnxruntime-web — vendored (voice)
==================================

Upstream:   https://www.npmjs.com/package/onnxruntime-web
Version:    1.22.0  (pinned to match what @moonshine-ai/moonshine-js@0.1.29
            fetches — Settings.BASE_ASSET_PATH.ONNX_RUNTIME)
License:    MIT
Source URL: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/onnxruntime-web@1.22.0/dist/

Files
-----
  ort-wasm-simd-threaded.jsep.mjs    ORT wasm loader (ESM). Builds its
                                     worker + .wasm URLs from import.meta.url.
  ort-wasm-simd-threaded.jsep.wasm   the wasm backend (SIMD + threads + JSEP
                                     WebGPU). ~21MB.
  SHA256SUMS                         checksums of the above (tamper floor).

Why these ship in-package
-------------------------
Moonshine bundles the ORT *JS* but fetches the wasm-side loader + binary
at runtime from jsdelivr. Under the MV3 extension_pages CSP
(script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'), ORT cannot import()/new Worker()
a CROSS-ORIGIN script, so it fetches the source and re-wraps it as a
blob: — which the same CSP then blocks (no blob: in script-src, and
Chrome refuses to let extension_pages add one). The wasm backend never
loads and voice dies ("not supported").

Serving ORT SAME-ORIGIN from the package fixes it: the loader resolves
its worker + wasm from import.meta.url, so with a same-origin base every
import()/new Worker()/new URL(...wasm) is same-origin — allowed by
'self', no blob, no network. peerd points Moonshine here via
Settings.BASE_ASSET_PATH.ONNX_RUNTIME in
peerd-runtime/voice/transcriber.js (init). crossOriginIsolated is true
(manifest COEP require-corp + COOP same-origin), so ORT threads with
same-origin pthread workers; proxy defaults false.

Update procedure
----------------
Bump only in lockstep with the ORT version Moonshine references (grep
the moonshine bundle for `onnxruntime-web@`). Re-download the jsep
.mjs + .wasm pair for that version, refresh SHA256SUMS, and smoke-test
(load unpacked → enable voice → transcribe — needs a real mic, can't be
verified headlessly).
